rulings.Rulings: continued to 12/4/19. new notice must be mailed for the new hearing date as the notice of hearing for the amended petition was sent 10/28/19 for a hearing on 11/6/19, which is less than the required 15 days notice. no later than noon on 11/27/19, petitioner must drop off a file stamped copy of the amended petiiton and a proposed order in room 202. plesae note-->faxed or emailed copies are NOT accepted. as a preliminary observation, mailed notice will be required to the FTB unless petitioner files a confirmation letter received from the FTB. in addition, a supplement must be filed that states whether the decedent was the surviving spouse of a person who received benefits, as if so, notice must be mailed under probate code 9202(a).

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE MACPRIN TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion By Prince M. Edmonds, Jr. To Quash Subpoenas
rulings.Rulings: This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Joseph M. Quinn. Respondent/Objector Prince M. Edmonds, Jr.'s Motion to Quash Subpoenas is GRANTED IN PART and DENIED IN PART. The Motion to Quash is granted as to the subpoenas addressed to Kaiser Permanente, Linda Mar Rehabilitation, St. Mary's Medical Center, UCSF Medical Center, Farmers New World Life Insurance Company and FedEx Pension Plan. Respondent/Objector has established a prima facie case that the discovery sought is not relevant to the subject matter involved in this probate proceeding or to determination if any motion in this proceeding in that the responses would be themselves admissible in evidence or reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence and Petitioner La Tonya Evette Carpenter has failed to show the subpoenaed information is reasonably relevant to her petition in this probate action. (See Code Civ. Proc., § 2017.010.) As to these third parties, the motion is granted on the separate and independent grounds of privacy. Respondent/Objector has a legally protected privacy interest in his medical and financial records and Petitioner has not shown that the evidence sought is "directly relevant" to the claims or defenses at issue in this probate proceeding. (See Tylo v. Superior Court (1997) 55 Cal.App.4th 1379, 1388; Britt v. Superior Court (1978) 20 Cal.3d 844, 863-864; Davis v. Superior Court (1992) 7 Cal.App.4th 1008, 1017-1018.) As to the subpoena addressed to Wells Fargo Bank, the motion is denied. Wells Fargo already produced the records and Petitioner's counsel has reviewed them. Thus, as to Wells Fargo Bank, the motion is moot. The procedural objections to the motion are overruled. The infirmities are excusable and, in any event, Petitioner has not demonstrated prejudice and no substantial prejudice is apparent on this record. Going forward, however, the parties will be expected to satisfy all requirements for discovery motions and waivers of procedural infirmities are not contemplated. Respondent/Objector Prince M. Edmonds, Jr. shall lodge with the clerk in Department 204 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order consistent with this tentative. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on November 5, 2019.
